Name: Application instance property lock disabled
Description:
This indicator checks for applications that have app instance property lock disabled.

Required permissions

  • Application.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
Application instance property lock is a feature in Entra ID that restricts the ability to add or modify sensitive properties on service principals.

Attackers can look for privileged service principals, and attempt to use them to move laterally, including by adding credentials onto the existing service principal, if they gain access to a user with the ability to do so. App instance property lock can prevent this.

References

How to configure app instance property lock in your applications - Microsoft identity platform | Microsoft Learn
ResultMessage: Found {0} applications that have a sensitive property lock disabled.
Remediation:
Enable the application instance lock feature and all of its properties for app registrations.

To enable the instance property lock of an application, Sign in to Microsoft Entra admin center as at least a Cloud Application Administrator. In the Applications tab, click on App Registrations and select the application to be configured. Select Authentication, and then select Configure under the App instance property lock section. In the App instance property lock pane, enter the settings for the lock.
